skill.name: pubmed-database
skill.description: Build advanced PubMed searches and direct NCBI E-utilities API workflows for biomedical literature research.
skill.summary: Search PubMed with Boolean, MeSH, citation, and E-utilities guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, credential, and blocker patterns, but review shows they are mostly Markdown PubMed query examples, placeholder API-key documentation, and biomedical or HTTP terminology. The only confirmed risk factor is intentional outbound network access to official PubMed and NCBI resources, which is expected for this skill.

low_findings.title: Intentional Network Access to NCBI APIs
low_findings.description: The skill teaches HTTP requests to NCBI E-utilities and links to PubMed or NCBI documentation. This is expected for a PubMed API skill, but users should understand that workflows may make outbound requests to official NCBI services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show direct REST API examples using the official NCBI E-utilities base URL. This confirms network behavior, but the destination and purpose match the skill description.

low_findings.title: Placeholder API Key Mentions Are Not Secret Access
low_findings.description: Static env-access findings are false positives. The files describe the NCBI api_key request parameter and use YOUR_API_KEY placeholders, with no evidence of reading environment variables, collecting credentials, or transmitting secrets to an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation placeholders and request parameters only. No process environment access or credential exfiltration pattern was found in the cited context.

low_findings.title: Markdown Query Blocks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The many external command findings are false positives from Markdown code fences and inline backticks. The examples contain PubMed search syntax, field tags, and query templates rather than executable shell, Ruby, or subprocess calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected examples are biomedical search strings inside Markdown fences. They are not scripts and do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: Blocker Pattern Findings Are Domain-Term False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance blocker findings are not confirmed. The cited contexts describe PubMed fields, clinical query categories, HTTP status codes, and support resources, not cryptographic algorithms or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines match benign documentation contexts. No evidence of weak cryptographic implementation, scanning, or system reconnaissance was found.

content.user_title: Search PubMed With Advanced Query Support
content.value_statement: Biomedical literature searches can miss key studies when queries are too broad or poorly structured. This skill helps build PubMed, MeSH, citation, and E-utilities workflows for repeatable research.
actual_capabilities: Constructs PubMed Boolean queries with field tags, phrase searches, wildcards, and date filters.
Explains MeSH terms, subheadings, major-topic limits, and publication type filters.
Guides ESearch, EFetch, ESummary, EPost, ELink, EInfo, EGQuery, ESpell, and ECitMatch usage.
Provides query templates for systematic reviews, clinical trials, epidemiology, and population studies.
Supports citation lookup by PMID, DOI, author, journal, year, volume, and page details.
limitations: Does not provide paid full-text access when PubMed only has citation or abstract records.
Requires users to supply and protect their own NCBI API key for higher rate limits.
NCBI rate limits still apply, especially for automated or large batch workflows.
Focuses on PubMed and Entrez workflows, not every biomedical database.

use_cases.title: Plan a systematic review search
use_cases.description: Translate a PICO question into PubMed queries with MeSH terms, synonyms, filters, and reproducible search notes.

use_cases.title: Automate citation retrieval
use_cases.description: Design an E-utilities workflow that searches PubMed, retrieves PMIDs, and fetches abstracts in controlled batches.

use_cases.title: Monitor new publications
use_cases.description: Create precise saved searches and alert strategies for journals, topics, authors, or clinical study types.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent articles
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me search PubMed for recent articles about asthma treatment in children. Include useful field tags and date filters.

prompt_templates.title: Build a MeSH query
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PubMed query for type 2 diabetes drug therapy using MeSH terms, synonyms, and randomized trial filters.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve citations with E-utilities
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ESearch and EFetch workflow for retrieving PubMed abstracts for a query, including batching and rate-limit handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design a systematic review workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this PICO question into a reproducible PubMed strategy with MeSH terms, title and abstract synonyms, exclusions, and documentation notes.

output_examples.input: Find recent systematic reviews about breast cancer screening.
output_examples.output: A refined PubMed search strategy using breast cancer synonyms, screening terms, systematic review filters, English language limits, and a recent publication date range.

output_examples.input: I have several PMIDs and need abstracts for analysis.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step EFetch workflow that groups PMIDs into batches, requests abstract text, observes NCBI rate limits, and records retrieval dates.

output_examples.input: Create a saved search for new randomized trials about metformin.
output_examples.output: A focused query using drug name and trial publication type filters, plus guidance for My NCBI alerts and periodic review.

best_practices: Combine MeSH terms with title and abstract synonyms for balanced recall and precision.
Document every search date, filter, field tag, and query revision for reproducibility.
Use batching, caching, and backoff when retrieving records through E-utilities.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a single keyword when a topic has MeSH terms and common synonyms.
Do not run high-volume API workflows without respecting NCBI rate limits.
Do not assume PubMed abstracts are equivalent to full-text article access.

faq.question: Does this skill search PubMed directly?
faq.answer: It provides query and API workflow guidance. Actual searches depend on the user or tool making PubMed or NCBI requests.

faq.question: Do I need an NCBI API key?
faq.answer: An API key is optional for basic use, but NCBI recommends it for higher E-utilities rate limits.

faq.question: Can it help with systematic reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports PICO framing, MeSH expansion, synonyms, filters, and search documentation.

faq.question: Can it retrieve full-text papers?
faq.answer: It can help find full-text links, but access depends on PubMed Central, publishers, institutions, or subscriptions.

faq.question: Which tools are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover PubMed search syntax and NCBI E-utilities such as ESearch, EFetch, ESummary, EPost, and ELink.

faq.question: Is this limited to biomedical literature?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for PubMed, MEDLINE, life sciences literature, and related Entrez database workflows.
